description: blackbox repo <path> — single-repo deep dive. onefetch-inspired: language breakdown via git2, top files changed, time invested all-time, PR history from DB, branch activity summary, pretty + JSON output.

userStories.title: Repo path resolution and validation
userStories.description: Resolve the user-supplied path to an absolute canonical path, verify it is a git repo, and look up the normalized form used in the DB (which may differ if the user passed a worktree path or a relative path). Returns an error if the path is not a git repo. Returns a warning (not an error) if the repo has never been seen in the DB — output will show zeros for activity data.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn resolve_repo_path(input: &str) -> anyhow::Result<std::path::PathBuf>` in a new `src/repo_deep_dive.rs` module — canonicalizes the path, verifies git2::Repository::open() succeeds, returns the canonical PathBuf
If git2::Repository::open fails: return Err with message 'Not a git repository: <path>'
If path does not exist: return Err with message 'Path not found: <path>'

The resolved path is then matched against `repo_path` column in `git_activity` using both exact match and prefix match (to handle worktree variants): `SELECT DISTINCT repo_path FROM git_activity WHERE repo_path = ?1 OR repo_path LIKE ?1 || '/%' LIMIT 1`
`pub fn find_db_repo_path(conn: &Connection, canonical: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Option<String>>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs` — returns the DB's repo_path string if found, None if never tracked
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: DB query: all-time activity for a single repo
userStories.description: Add a query function that returns all git_activity, review_activity, and ai_sessions rows for a specific repo_path (no time filter). This is the raw data layer for the deep dive — all subsequent computed fields use it.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn query_repo_all_time(conn: &Connection, repo_path: &str) -> anyhow::Result<RepoAllTimeData>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`

`pub struct RepoAllTimeData` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs` with fields: `repo_path: String`, `events: Vec<crate::query::ActivityEvent>`, `reviews: Vec<crate::query::ReviewInfo>`, `ai_sessions: Vec<crate::query::AiSessionInfo>`
events: all rows from git_activity WHERE repo_path = ?1, ordered by timestamp ASC
reviews: all rows from review_activity WHERE repo_path = ?1, ordered by reviewed_at ASC
ai_sessions: all rows from ai_sessions WHERE repo_path = ?1, ordered by started_at ASC
If repo_path not in DB: all three vecs are empty (not an error)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Language breakdown via git2
userStories.description: Walk the repo's HEAD tree using git2, count lines (or files as a proxy) per file extension, map extensions to language names, sort by line count descending. Binary files are excluded. This is a pure git2 operation — no filesystem walk, so it works on any tracked repo.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct LanguageBreakdown` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`: `language: String`, `file_count: usize`, `line_count: usize`, `percent: f64`
`pub fn compute_language_breakdown(repo_path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<LanguageBreakdown>>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`
Uses git2::Repository::open(repo_path), repo.head(), head.peel_to_tree(), then tree.walk() to enumerate all blob entries
For each blob entry: get file extension from entry name; if no extension or extension is empty, skip. Call repo.find_blob(entry.id()) to get the blob; if blob.is_binary() is true, skip it
Count lines per extension: blob.content() → split on b'\n' → count lines

Extension-to-language map (hardcoded `match` in a helper fn): rs→Rust, ts→TypeScript, tsx→TypeScript, js→JavaScript, jsx→JavaScript, py→Python, go→Go, java→Java, kt→Kotlin, swift→Swift, rb→Ruby, c→C, cpp→C++, cc→C++, h→C/C++ Header, cs→C#, sh→Shell, bash→Shell, zsh→Shell, fish→Shell, toml→TOML, yaml→YAML, yml→YAML, json→JSON, md→Markdown, html→HTML, css→CSS, scss→CSS, sql→SQL. Unknown extensions: use the extension as-is (lowercase).
Aggregate by language name (multiple extensions can map to same language), compute percent = line_count / total_lines * 100.0
Sort result by line_count descending. Return empty vec if HEAD has no tree or repo has no commits
If git2::Repository::open fails, return empty vec (not an error — caller already validated)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Top files changed query
userStories.description: Walk commit history from HEAD using git2 (or query from DB commit hashes), compute which files appear most frequently in diffs. Returns top N files by change frequency. This uses git2 diff operations and is bounded by commit count.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct FileChurnEntry` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`: `path: String`, `change_count: usize`
`pub fn compute_top_files(repo_path: &Path, limit: usize) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<FileChurnEntry>>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`
Open repo with git2::Repository::open(repo_path). If repo has no commits (empty repo), return empty vec
Walk commits from HEAD using revwalk with Sort::TIME, cap at 500 commits to bound runtime

For each commit: get diff from parent (or empty tree if first commit) via repo.diff_tree_to_tree(). For each diff delta: increment count for delta.new_file().path() (as string). Skip binary deltas (delta.new_file().is_binary())
Sort by change_count descending, take `limit` entries
Cap total walk at 500 commits; log::debug if capped
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Time invested: all-time estimate for single repo
userStories.description: Compute total estimated time invested in a repo using all tracked events (no time window). Reuses existing estimate_time_v2() logic from query.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn compute_time_invested(data: &RepoAllTimeData, session_gap_minutes: u64, first_commit_minutes: u64) -> chrono::Duration` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`
Builds ai_intervals from data.ai_sessions (no clipping — use full session duration: start=started_at, end=ended_at.unwrap_or(now))
Calls crate::query::estimate_time_v2(&data.events, &ai_intervals, session_gap_minutes, first_commit_minutes)
Returns the Duration
If data.events is empty and data.ai_sessions is empty: returns Duration::zero()
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: PR history for single repo
userStories.description: Enumerate all PRs ever associated with this repo, combining: (a) review_activity rows from DB, (b) live gh CLI fetch. This gives a richer picture than the time-windowed query in enrichment.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct RepoPrEntry` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`: `number: u64`, `title: String`, `state: String`, `branch: Option<String>`, `url: Option<String>`
`pub fn fetch_repo_pr_history(repo_path: &str, data: &RepoAllTimeData) -> Vec<RepoPrEntry>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`
Step 1: collect unique PR numbers from data.reviews, create RepoPrEntry with title from review_activity, state='REVIEWED', url=None, branch=None

Step 2: if gh_available() (import from enrichment.rs or duplicate the OnceLock check), run `gh pr list --state all --json number,title,state,headRefName,url --limit 30` in repo_path dir (same pattern as enrichment.rs fetch_prs). Parse as Vec<serde_json::Value>
Step 3: merge — for each gh PR, upsert into the map by number (gh data takes precedence over review-only entries). Add any new PRs not in step 1
Sort by number descending (most recent first). Return empty vec if no gh and no reviews
Graceful degradation: if gh command fails, return review-only list
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Branch activity summary
userStories.description: From git_activity events for this repo, summarize branch activity: list branches seen, commit count per branch, last active timestamp per branch.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct BranchActivity` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`: `name: String`, `commit_count: usize`, `last_active: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>`
`pub fn compute_branch_activity(data: &RepoAllTimeData) -> Vec<BranchActivity>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs`
Scan data.events: for each event with event_type='commit' and branch=Some(name), increment commit_count for that branch and track max timestamp
Also include branches from branch_switch events (branch=Some(name)) even if commit_count=0 for those
Sort by last_active descending
Return empty vec if no events
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: RepoDeepDive aggregate struct
userStories.description: Bundle all computed deep-dive data into one struct. This is what the output layer renders.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct RepoDeepDive` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs` with fields: `repo_path: String`, `repo_name: String`, `total_commits: usize`, `first_commit_at: Option<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>>`, `last_commit_at: Option<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>>`, `total_estimated_time: chrono::Duration`, `languages: Vec<LanguageBreakdown>`, `top_files: Vec<FileChurnEntry>`, `branches: Vec<BranchActivity>`, `prs: Vec<RepoPrEntry>`, `tracked: bool`
`tracked: bool` = false when find_db_repo_path returned None (repo never seen in DB)
first_commit_at / last_commit_at: min/max timestamp from data.events where event_type='commit'. None if no commits
total_commits: count of events with event_type='commit' in RepoAllTimeData

`pub fn build_deep_dive(repo_path: &str, conn: &Connection, config: &crate::config::Config) -> anyhow::Result<RepoDeepDive>` in `src/repo_deep_dive.rs` — orchestrates all sub-computations: resolve_repo_path, find_db_repo_path, query_repo_all_time, compute_language_breakdown, compute_top_files, compute_time_invested, compute_branch_activity, fetch_repo_pr_history
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Pretty output for repo deep dive
userStories.description: Render RepoDeepDive as a human-readable terminal display inspired by onefetch: repo name header, language bar, top stats in two columns, then sections for branches, top files, PR history.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn render_repo_pretty(dive: &RepoDeepDive)` in `src/output.rs`
Header: `=== <repo_name> ===` bold cyan. If !tracked: add `(untracked — no activity in DB)` dimmed

Stats block (each on own line, label right-padded to 20 chars): `Tracked commits`, `Total time`, `First commit`, `Last commit`. Dates formatted as `%Y-%m-%d`. Time via format_duration(). If None: show `-`

Languages section header `Languages:` bold. For each LanguageBreakdown (top 8): `  <language> <percent:.1>%` with a mini bar rendered as colored blocks. Bar: 20 chars wide, filled = round(percent/100*20) chars as `█`, empty as `░`. Color: cycle through [cyan, green, yellow, magenta, blue, red, white, bright_cyan]
Top files section header `Top files changed:` bold. For each FileChurnEntry (top 10): `  <change_count>x <path>` — count right-padded to 4 chars
Branches section header `Branches:` bold. For each BranchActivity: `  <name> — <commit_count> commits, last active <YYYY-MM-DD>`

PRs section header `Pull requests:` bold. Only shown if prs is non-empty. For each RepoPrEntry (top 10 by number): `  #<number> <title> [<state>]`. State colors: MERGED=magenta, OPEN=green, CLOSED=dimmed, REVIEWED=cyan
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: JSON output for repo deep dive
userStories.description: Serialize RepoDeepDive to JSON. Reuse the --format OutputFormat enum pattern established in Today/Week/Month commands.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct JsonRepoDeepDive` in `src/output.rs` with serde::Serialize — mirrors RepoDeepDive but with chrono fields as RFC3339 strings and Duration as minutes (i64)
`pub fn render_repo_json(dive: &RepoDeepDive) -> String` in `src/output.rs` — constructs JsonRepoDeepDive and calls serde_json::to_string_pretty()
LanguageBreakdown, FileChurnEntry, BranchActivity, RepoPrEntry all derive serde::Serialize (add derives in repo_deep_dive.rs)
All Option<DateTime> fields serialize as Option<String> (rfc3339 or null)
Duration fields serialize as `*_minutes: i64`
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: CLI command wiring: blackbox repo <path>
userStories.description: Add the `repo` subcommand to the Commands enum and wire it in main.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add variant to Commands enum in cli.rs: `/// Single-repo deep dive (language breakdown, top files, time invested, PR history)` / `Repo { #[arg()] path: String, #[arg(long, default_value = "pretty")] format: OutputFormat }`
Add match arm in main.rs: open DB, call build_deep_dive(), dispatch to render_repo_pretty() or render_repo_json()

main.rs match arm for Commands::Repo: load config (for session_gap/first_commit params), open DB, call `blackbox::repo_deep_dive::build_deep_dive(&path, &conn, &config)?`, then match format: Pretty → render_repo_pretty(&dive), Json → println!("{}", render_repo_json(&dive)), Csv → println!("{}", render_repo_json(&dive)) (JSON fallback for CSV)
Add `pub mod repo_deep_dive;` to `src/lib.rs`
`cargo build` passes
`blackbox repo .` runs without panic on any git repo

userStories.title: Unit tests: resolve_repo_path and find_db_repo_path
userStories.description: Tests in tests/repo_deep_dive_test.rs covering path resolution, git repo detection, and DB lookup.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: resolve_repo_path with a valid git repo temp dir → returns canonical PathBuf, Ok
Test: resolve_repo_path with a non-existent path → Err containing 'Path not found'
Test: resolve_repo_path with a non-git temp dir → Err containing 'Not a git repository'
Test: find_db_repo_path on empty DB → returns Ok(None)
Test: find_db_repo_path after inserting a row with that repo_path → returns Ok(Some(path))
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: compute_language_breakdown
userStories.description: Tests for language breakdown using real git2 repos initialized in temp dirs.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: repo with one committed .rs file containing 3 lines → languages contains LanguageBreakdown { language: 'Rust', line_count: 3, percent: 100.0, file_count: 1 }
Test: repo with one .rs file and one .py file → two entries, percents sum to ~100.0
Test: repo with a binary file (write 3 null bytes) → binary file excluded, does not appear in breakdown
Test: empty repo (no commits) → returns empty vec without error
Test: file with no extension → skipped
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: compute_top_files and compute_branch_activity
userStories.description: Tests for file churn and branch activity using git2 repos in temp dirs.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: repo with 3 commits all touching the same file → FileChurnEntry { path: 'file.rs', change_count: 3 } is first result
Test: repo with 0 commits → compute_top_files returns empty vec

Test: compute_branch_activity on RepoAllTimeData with two commit events on 'main' and one on 'feature/x' → BranchActivity for 'main' has commit_count=2, for 'feature/x' has commit_count=1, sorted by last_active desc
Test: compute_branch_activity on empty events → returns empty vec
All tests pass

userStories.title: Integration test: blackbox repo CLI end-to-end
userStories.description: CLI integration test using assert_cmd: run blackbox repo against a real git repo path and verify output format.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: `blackbox repo <valid_git_repo_path>` → exits 0, stdout contains repo name
Test: `blackbox repo <valid_git_repo_path> --format json` → exits 0, stdout is valid JSON with keys: repo_name, total_commits, total_estimated_minutes, languages, top_files, branches
Test: `blackbox repo /tmp/definitely-not-a-repo` → exits non-zero, stderr or stdout contains 'Not a git repository' or 'Path not found'
Test: `blackbox repo <valid_git_repo_path>` on a repo not in the DB → exits 0, output contains 'untracked' indicator
All tests pass

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
